name: Mondariz Loop
description: Tackle the difficult Mondariz Return, a 31.9-mile (51.3 km) road cycling route with 3400 feet (1036 metres) of elevation gain.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Mondariz-Balneario traverse the verdant River Tea valley, characterized by lush countryside and rolling hills. The terrain offers varied elevation profiles, from gentle slopes along the river to more demanding ascents into the surrounding mountains. Cyclists can expect mostly paved surfaces winding through scenic landscapes, featuring trickling streams and historic Roman bridges. Best road cycling routes around Mondariz-Balneario The most popular road cycling route is Mondariz Loop, a difficult 31.9 miles (51.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 52 minutes to complete. This route offers significant elevation gain through the region's varied landscape. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Fountain of the Village of Festín – Mondariz Spa loop from O Condado, a moderate 37.4 miles (60.2 km) path. This route explores the area's natural beauty, including the renowned Mondariz Spa. Local road cyclists also love the Remedios Bridge – Footbridge loop from Ponteareas, a 71.2 miles (114.6 km) trail leading through the River Tea valley, often completed in about 6 hours 25 minutes. Road cycling around Mondariz-Balneario is defined by the scenic River Tea valley, rolling hills, and historic Roman bridges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes with significant climbs. The routes in Mondariz-Balneario are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.7 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Mondariz-Balneario's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Mondariz-Balneario?
A: There are over 50 road cycling routes documented around Mondariz-Balneario on komoot, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Mondariz-Balneario?
A: Road cycling routes in Mondariz-Balneario primarily feature mostly paved surfaces. You'll encounter varied terrain, from gentle slopes winding through the verdant River Tea valley to more demanding ascents into the surrounding rolling hills and mountains like Picaraña, Galleiro, and Alto do Marco.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Mondariz-Balneario?
A: Yes, while many routes offer significant climbs, there are 32 moderate routes available that are suitable for those with good basic fitness. These routes provide a great way to experience the region's beauty without extreme difficulty. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31947611" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain of the Village of Festín – Mondariz Spa loop from O Condado&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 37.4-mile path.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes in the area?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Mondariz-Balneario offers 23 difficult routes with significant elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20151614" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Remedios Bridge – Footbridge loop from Ponteareas&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 71.2-mile route with over 2,200 meters of elevation gain, typically taking over six hours to complete. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Remedios Bridge – Cernadela Bridge loop from O Condado&lt;/a&gt;, covering 94.6 kilometers with over 2,000 meters of ascent.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Mondariz-Balneario is rich in history. Along your rides, you might encounter historical elements like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1283130" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge of the Partitions&lt;/a&gt;, known for its medieval characteristics, or the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1262296" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Remedios Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. The broader Condado region also features Roman buildings and ruins, adding a historical dimension to your cycling experience.

Q: Are there any natural viewpoints or scenic spots accessible by road bike?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its picturesque landscapes. You can find stunning views from locations like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2884977" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte San Nomedio Observation Tower&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2499686" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto San Nomedio Summit and Hermitage&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also wind through the scenic River Tea valley, offering views of trickling streams and lush countryside.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Mondariz-Balneario?
A: Given the region's verdant River Tea valley and lush countryside, spring, summer, and early autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling. These seasons provide comfortable temperatures and vibrant natural scenery, ideal for enjoying the varied terrain.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in Mondariz-Balneario?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer rides, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20151614" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Remedios Bridge – Footbridge loop from Ponteareas&lt;/a&gt; span over 114 kilometers, offering an extensive exploration of the River Tea valley and surrounding areas. Another long option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Remedios Bridge – Cernadela Bridge loop from O Condado&lt;/a&gt; at 94.6 kilometers.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Mondariz-Balneario?
A: The road cycling routes in Mondariz-Balneario are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 3.7 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, scenic River Tea valley, and the presence of historic Roman bridges that make for a unique riding experience.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Mondariz-Balneario are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e963174968/mondariz-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mondariz Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38279855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Remedios Bridge – Castillo de Sobroso loop from O Condado&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a 72.9-kilometer ride.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while cycling?
A: The region is characterized by its lush, green countryside, shaped by the River Tea. You'll cycle past trickling streams, river beaches, and through the scenic valley. Notable natural features include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5518672" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barragán River&lt;/a&gt; and various summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2532948" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte de A Picaraña&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6288387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Galleiro&lt;/a&gt;, providing diverse natural beauty.
tips: The Hotel Balneario de Mondariz is one of the best-known spas in Spain and is located next to the Tea River.
Stone bridge from Roman times over the river Tea. The surface of the bridge is made of stone and you may have to walk, but don't forget to imagine the Roman legions so many centuries ago.

faqs.question: Where does the Mondariz Return route start?
faqs.answer: The starting point for this route is conveniently located next to a parking area in Mondariz. This makes it easy to access if you're arriving by car.

faqs.question: Is the Mondariz Return suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, the Mondariz Return is classified as a difficult road cycling route. It demands a very good level of physical fitness due to significant elevation changes and its overall length. It's best suited for experienced cyclists looking for a challenging ride.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily composed of firmly paved tracks, making it ideal for road bikes. You can expect smooth surfaces suitable for high-performance cycling.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Mondariz Return route?
faqs.answer: Along the way, you'll pass by notable points of interest such as the historic Hotel Balneario de Mondariz and the charming Cernadela Bridge. The region is also known for its natural beauty, offering green landscapes and views of the Galician countryside.

faqs.question: Is the Mondariz Return a loop route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the name 'Mondariz Return' implies a loop, meaning it starts and ends in or around Mondariz, bringing you back to your starting point.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: While specific seasonal recommendations aren't provided, the general region of Galicia experiences mild winters and warm summers. Spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant cycling conditions with moderate temperatures and less extreme weather, making them ideal for tackling a challenging route like this.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with several other paths. You'll find it intersects with parts of the Sendeiro das Greas, Mtb Vía Mariana, Via Mariana, and Río Barragán, among others.

faqs.question: Is the Mondariz Return dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As a challenging road cycling route, it's not typically recommended for cycling with dogs. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure they are safely secured in a bike trailer or carrier, and always keep them on a leash if walking near roads.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Mondariz Return route. It's freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to refuel along the route?
faqs.answer: While specific cafes directly on the route aren't listed, Mondariz and surrounding towns in the province of Pontevedra typically offer various establishments where you can find refreshments and food. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially given the route's difficulty.

faqs.question: What kind of bike is best for the Mondariz Return?
faqs.answer: Given that the route is primarily on firmly paved tracks and is classified for 'racebike' sport, a road bike is the most suitable choice. Its lightweight frame and narrow tires are ideal for the smooth, challenging terrain.
